These rules may be railed the Ministers' (Allowances. Medical Treatment cmd other privileges) Amendment Rules, 1960. 2. In t h - Ministers' (Allowances, Medical Treatment and other privileges), Rules, 1957 (hereinafter referred to as the said rules), in rule 12, after sub-rule (2), the following sub^ru'e shall be inserted, namely:— "(3) A Minister, when travelling on duty by road, shall b > entitled, without payment, to take with him one relative." 3. In rule 13 oc the said rules,—(i) after sub rule (1), the following sub-rule shall be inserted, namely:— "(2) A Minister who does not utiliz1 the free transport provided by the Air Company between the air-booking Centre and the airport" mav also recover. In respect of the journey to or from the airport. actual travelling expenses or road mileage as for journey on dutv by road admissible under rule 12."; (ii) existing sub-rule (2) shall be renumbered as sub-rule (3). 4. In rule 23 of the said rules, after item (iii), the following items shall be inserted, namely:— "(iv) A Deputy Minister, when travelling on duty by road, shall be entitled without payment, to take one relative (v) In th - case of a journey hy air on duty, a Deputy Minister who does not utilize the free transport provided t>v the Air Company between the air-booking Centre and the airport shall be entitled to" draw mileage allowance in respect of the road journey between the "duty point" and the airport, C 7?r ) IV Q -- 1 of 2 -- 712 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA EXTRAORDINARY [PART II-SEC, S (i)] (vi) A Deputy Minister shall be entitled to draw the actual cost of hiring a conveyance for short road journeys on duty at his headquarters for which no travelling allowance is admissible to him under these rul s, subject to his furnishing a certificate in each case that such con- veyance was hired because a staff car cou.d not be made available by the Ministry concern _<d or taken on loan from any othef Ministry, (vii) The proviso to Supplementary Rule 46 does not apply to Deputy Ministers."
